reasoning: The initial probability anchor is derived from the FRED statistical tool, which assigns a ~0.35-0.37 probability of an increase by June 29 based on recent downward momentum and seasonal factors. Thus, for the short term (June 29 and July 22), the probabilities are set below 0.5, specifically at 0.34 and 0.40. Over the medium term (September), market expectations heavily price in a Fed rate cut (indicated by the ~60% FedWatch odds and the June FOMC dot plot detailing two cuts by 2025 year-end). When the Fed cuts rates, the 3-month yield rapidly declines, standardly triggering a bull steepening of the yield curve. This shifts the expectation to an inversion-busting increase, placing the September 20 probability above a coin-flip. Following the strict instruction that forecast uncertainty must mathematically increase over extended horizons, the absolute deviation from 0.5 is progressively shrunk for distant dates: June is 0.16 away from 0.5, July is 0.10, September is 0.08, and December is 0.05. This produces a compliant probability array that organically maps onto the macro roadmap while executing targeted mean-reversion.
